---
title: "Kata"
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

A personal problem archive — algorithms, single-cell biology, research design, one problem at a time.

## Problems

```{python}
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import HTML
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("registry.csv")
df["title"] = '<a href="' + df["quiz"] + '">' + df["title"] + "</a>"
df["solution"] = '<a href="' + df["solution"] + '">solution</a>'
df = df[["index", "title", "category", "description", "solution", "added"]]
df.columns = ["#", "Title", "Category", "Description", "Crack", "Added"]
HTML(df.to_html(escape=False, index=False))
```

Not every problem has an obvious answer — and that's the point. Each one is a small mystery waiting to be solutioned, one clue at a time. The archive grows slowly, but so does the intuition behind it. No rush. Sherlock didn't solve cases in a day either.
